title: Friends And Family

jokes.text: I rather not attach my name to a gift that I had no hand in selecting.
jokes.setup: Family giving father a gift together
jokes.punchline: David immediately distances himself from any gift he didn't personally curate
jokes.explanation: Perfect David character moment - his need to control and curate everything, even gift-giving credit

jokes.text: You all look like cartoons.
jokes.setup: Looking at family portrait
jokes.punchline: Roland's blunt assessment of their formal portrait
jokes.explanation: Roland's characteristic lack of tact delivers a solid visual joke

jokes.text: I don't even remember posing for this. Because you didn't. You were in rehab when we sat for this.
jokes.setup: Alexis confused about not remembering the portrait session
jokes.punchline: Reveals she was in rehab during the sitting
jokes.explanation: Great escalating reveal about their past wealth and dysfunction

jokes.text: I wasn't in rehab, I was at rehab visiting Stavros.
jokes.setup: Alexis defending herself against rehab accusation
jokes.punchline: Her distinction between being in rehab vs visiting someone in rehab
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis logic - the technicality that misses the point entirely

jokes.text: We had your face painted on the body of my assistant. That's why you look so good.
jokes.setup: Explaining how Alexis appears in the portrait despite being absent
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment that the assistant's body made her look better
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Moira moment - simultaneously solving the mystery and delivering a cutting insult

jokes.text: $20 bucks, I'll give you a hand taking it in. $30 buck, I'll help you in with it. $40 buck and I'll help you in with in. I can keep going up. Can play this game all day, pal.
jokes.setup: David needs help moving the portrait
jokes.punchline: Roland's increasingly expensive offers to help
jokes.explanation: Classic Roland opportunism, escalating absurdly but true to character

jokes.text: I can't tell if this room is just very, very small, or if the portrait is very, very big. Well, I think it's quite possible that both are true.
jokes.setup: David puzzled by the portrait's overwhelming presence
jokes.punchline: Patrick's logical assessment that both could be true
jokes.explanation: Solid visual comedy enhanced by Patrick's practical response

jokes.text: Maybe move some wigs.
jokes.setup: Johnny suggesting ways to make room for the portrait
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning moving Moira's wigs as furniture
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to Moira's wig collection being treated as decor

jokes.text: enormous strength of this family.
jokes.setup: Johnny praising the portrait as representing family strength
jokes.punchline: The visual irony of their current diminished circumstances
jokes.explanation: Gentle irony about their fall from grace

jokes.text: It's just that I don't normally share beverages with people. / Really. That is shocking news.
jokes.setup: David's germaphobic concern about sharing juice
jokes.punchline: Patrick's sarcastic response
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy with Patrick's deadpan response to David's neuroses.

jokes.text: Some people have nice, clean mouths, and some people have sloppy mouths.
jokes.setup: Patrick explaining why he doesn't share beverages
jokes.punchline: His bizarre categorization of mouth cleanliness
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully weird Patrick moment that reveals his particular quirks

jokes.text: I look to like Gwyneth who soft-launched the goop newsletter and now it's a thriving lifestyle publication slash empire.
jokes.setup: David justifying a soft launch strategy
jokes.punchline: Using Gwyneth Paltrow's questionable empire as business inspiration
jokes.explanation: Perfect David reference to wellness culture and his aspirational thinking

jokes.text: No. You've got a sloppy mouth.
jokes.setup: David asking for his juice back
jokes.punchline: Patrick using his own mouth classification against David
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the earlier mouth categories setup

jokes.text: She said that her cat is better. She doesn't have a cat.
jokes.setup: Alexis relaying a patient's message
jokes.punchline: Ted immediately catching her obvious mistake
jokes.explanation: Classic Alexis incompetence moment with Ted's exasperated correction

jokes.text: somebody isn't coming in today and someone's cat is better. Oh, and someone's something may be worse...
jokes.setup: Alexis trying to remember phone messages
jokes.punchline: Her increasingly vague and unhelpful recollection
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Alexis's scatterbrained message-taking

jokes.text: I got a 60 and a 65! Alexis, those are the class averages. You got a 63 and a 68!
jokes.setup: Alexis excitedly announcing her grades
jokes.punchline: She's confused the class averages with her actual scores
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis moment - celebrating the wrong numbers but still doing better than expected

jokes.text: I, in the mean time, will go to get back to... work-o.
jokes.setup: Ted trying to exit awkwardly after romantic tension
jokes.punchline: His made-up word 'work-o' as nervous filler
jokes.explanation: Ted's awkward verbal fumbling creates a quotable nonsense word

jokes.text: Did it grow?
jokes.setup: David seeing the portrait again
jokes.punchline: The portrait seems impossibly large in their small space
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective visual comedy about the portrait's overwhelming size

jokes.text: It's always better to go under. Go under? No. When it comes to expectations It's always to go under than over.
jokes.setup: David explaining soft launch strategy
jokes.punchline: His poor word choice suggests business failure
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection play on 'going under' in business context

jokes.text: Every Rose video we opened had fireworks. Day or night. People love pizzazz.
jokes.setup: Johnny sharing business wisdom from their past
jokes.punchline: The absurd excess of fireworks for video store openings
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to their over-the-top wealthy past and Johnny's business style

jokes.text: Does that mean we get a 50 percent discount?
jokes.setup: Johnny asking about friends and family discount logic
jokes.punchline: His misunderstanding that discounts would stack
jokes.explanation: Johnny's business logic gone wrong in a relatable way

jokes.text: this modest little vigil you're hosting
jokes.setup: Moira commenting on David's store opening
jokes.punchline: Describing it as a 'vigil' instead of celebration
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Moira vocabulary choice that perfectly captures her dramatic flair and David's understated approach

jokes.text: Like when your parents kiss their friends... on the mouth. Yes. Like kissing your parents.
jokes.setup: Trying to normalize their kiss as adult friendship
jokes.punchline: Making increasingly uncomfortable comparisons
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy as they make their romantic tension worse by over-explaining

jokes.text: I had to delete groupon from my phone last week. Just 'cause three vacuums is enough... Vacuums.
jokes.setup: Alexis admitting she loves discounts
jokes.punchline: Her compulsive vacuum buying problem
jokes.explanation: Great Alexis character detail - her shopping addiction revealing itself in unexpected ways

jokes.text: Back to work-o.
jokes.setup: Ted trying to exit awkwardly again
jokes.punchline: Repeating his made-up word from earlier
jokes.explanation: Good callback to his earlier verbal fumbling

jokes.text: I've been meaning to have my family portrait airlifted in...
jokes.setup: Johnny explaining family portraits in businesses
jokes.punchline: Patrick's dry comment about the portrait's massive size
jokes.explanation: Perfect Patrick deadpan delivery about the portrait's absurd size

jokes.text: I don't think that's gonna happen.
jokes.setup: Johnny saying they won't notice the portrait with time
jokes.punchline: Patrick's matter-of-fact disagreement
jokes.explanation: Classic Patrick honesty cutting through Johnny's optimism

jokes.text: You'd think there'd be more of a market for oversized paintings of other people's families.
jokes.setup: Johnny explaining no one wanted to buy the portrait
jokes.punchline: Patrick's sarcastic observation about the niche market
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Patrick line - perfectly captures the absurdity while staying in character

jokes.text: She paid extra for that.
jokes.setup: Patrick noting Moira's eyes following him in the portrait
jokes.punchline: Johnny casually revealing this was an expensive upgrade
jokes.explanation: Great reveal about their past wealth and Moira's vanity - paying extra for creepy portrait eyes

jokes.text: Who's Gwen?
jokes.setup: Roland asking about plus-ones for Gwen
jokes.punchline: David's confused response to this unknown person
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective confusion about his guest list spiraling out of control

jokes.text: Twyla's whole family. So, like, 75?
jokes.setup: David asking how many people are coming
jokes.punchline: Patrick's casual mention of Twyla's enormous family
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the soft launch gone wrong with specific absurd number

jokes.text: Looks like this soft launch is firming up a bit, huh. But it's not supposed to be firm. Well, with this many people it's definitely at least semi-firm. Okay. Well, as long as it doesn't get hard.
jokes.setup: Patrick commenting on the growing guest list
jokes.punchline: Their extended metaphor with unintended sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Solid innuendo comedy that builds naturally from business metaphor

jokes.text: I'm Darleen's cousin. Who the fuck are you?
jokes.setup: David questioning someone in line at his store
jokes.punchline: Aggressive response from random extended family member
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how out of control the guest list has become

jokes.text: That kind of language, folks, will not be tolerated at Rose Apothecary. Thank you. This is a safe place.
jokes.setup: Customer swearing at David
jokes.punchline: David's ineffective attempt to maintain store decorum
jokes.explanation: Perfect David trying to maintain his curated brand image while chaos erupts

jokes.text: I for one blame Gwyneth.
jokes.setup: David stressed about the overwhelming crowd
jokes.punchline: Blaming his earlier Gwyneth Paltrow business inspiration
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to his earlier aspirational business reference, now turned scapegoat

jokes.text: He's managed to create in this town something truly winsome. I would shop here, John. Even without a nagging sense of obligation.
jokes.setup: Moira praising David's store
jokes.punchline: Admitting she'd only shop there out of family obligation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira moment - elaborate praise that accidentally reveals her true motivations

jokes.text: Um, I'm pretty sure your hands are gonna be fine.
jokes.setup: Customer worried about using foot cream on hands
jokes.punchline: David's barely concealed exasperation at the silly question
jokes.explanation: Good David retail patience wearing thin moment

jokes.text: Is it drugs? Uh, no. It's loose leaf tea. Okay. Because it smells exactly like... Oh, come on! Wow! Geez, I didn't realize this place was a front.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn suspicious about tea product
jokes.punchline: Her immediate assumption David is running a drug operation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of small-town suspicions about David's sophisticated products

jokes.text: I started drinking tea in high school.
jokes.setup: Discussion about the suspicious tea
jokes.punchline: Roland's obvious euphemism for marijuana use
jokes.explanation: Standard weed joke but fits Roland's character

jokes.text: Actually I was talking about you passing your exams.
jokes.setup: Ted mentioning big news, Alexis thinks it's about their romantic moment
jokes.punchline: Ted was actually talking about her academic achievement
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that highlights Alexis's self-centered thinking

jokes.text: I had nothing but doubts... No doubts. That you could do this.
jokes.setup: Moira trying to congratulate Alexis
jokes.punchline: Her verbal slip revealing her actual doubts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira moment - her attempt at support revealing her true feelings

jokes.text: we could cut our faces out and make four smaller, individual portraits.
jokes.setup: Johnny suggesting solutions for the oversized portrait
jokes.punchline: His bizarre idea to dismember the family portrait
jokes.explanation: Good absurd solution that misses the point entirely

jokes.text: You picked up a stick. No, it's a pencil shaped like a twig.
jokes.setup: Ted giving Alexis a graduation gift
jokes.punchline: The gift is absurdly simple and Alexis's blunt assessment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ted's earnest simplicity vs Alexis's directness

jokes.text: I hate it when they don't put price tags on things.
jokes.setup: Ted mentioning the pencil was expensive
jokes.punchline: His relatable complaint about unclear pricing
jokes.explanation: Relatable Ted moment about being surprised by David's boutique pricing

jokes.text: I think maybe we should just avoid kisses for a while. Even adult ones.
jokes.setup: After another accidental kiss
jokes.punchline: Ted's overly specific rules about their adult kissing arrangement
jokes.explanation: Good callback to their earlier awkward rationalization

jokes.text: we'd be 25 percent richer if we just done a hard launch.
jokes.setup: Reflecting on the successful store opening
jokes.punchline: Patrick's practical observation about the discount's impact
jokes.explanation: Good Patrick practicality cutting through the celebration

jokes.text: Oh, they are.
jokes.setup: Alexis noting the portrait's eyes following them
jokes.punchline: David confirming the creepy eyes are real
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the portrait's creepy feature for final visual gag

tmdb_overview: Johnny and Moira struggle to find a place for their family portrait. David hosts a 'soft launch' of his new store.
insight: Bittersweet character comedy sustains 47 jokes across emotional family reckoning.
